connectorUiConfig.title: BitSight Security Statistics (via Codeless Connector Framework)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: The [BitSight](https://www.bitsight.com/) data connector provides the capability to ingest security statistics, company profiles, rating details, diligence history, risk vector statistics, and vulnerability data from your BitSight portfolio into Microsoft Sentinel through the BitSight REST API. Refer to the [BitSight API documentation](https://help.bitsighttech.com/hc/en-us/articles/115014888388-API-Token-Management) for more information.

sampleQueries.description: Get sample of BitSight Company Details
Get company security ratings over time
sampleQueries.query:
{{graphQueriesTableName}}
 | where TimeGenerated > ago(90d)
 | summarize LatestRating = arg_max(TimeGenerated, CurrentRating) by Name
 | order by LatestRating asc

sampleQueries.description: Get sample of BitSight Company Rating Details
Get findings summary with latest data per company/stat

dataTypes.name: {{graphQueriesTableName}}
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
{{graphQueriesTableName}}
 | where TimeGenerated > ago(12h)
 | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
 | where isnotempty(Time)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and Write permissions are required.

customs.name: BitSight API Token
customs.description: A BitSight API Token is required to authenticate requests to the BitSight REST API. [See the documentation](https://help.bitsighttech.com/hc/en-us/articles/115014888388-API-Token-Management) to learn more about API Token management.

instructionSteps.title: 1. Connection Management
instructionSteps.description: Manage your BitSight statistics data stream connections

parameters.content:
## BitSight Statistics Connections

Manage multiple BitSight statistics connections. Each connection selects one or more **data streams** to ingest and assigns a **Connection Name** stored in the `connectionName` column of every ingested record.

> **Authentication**: BitSight uses HTTP Basic Authentication where the API token is used as **both** the username and password.

parameters.label: Add Connection
parameters.title: Add BitSight Statistics Connection
parameters.subtitle: Configure a new BitSight statistics connection

parameters.content:
## 1. Select Data Streams

Choose which BitSight statistics data types to collect for this connection. You can select multiple streams.

## 3. Authentication

BitSight uses your API token as **both** the username and password for HTTP Basic Authentication.

parameters.label: BitSight API Token (Password)
parameters.placeholder: Paste your BitSight API Token again
parameters.name: password

parameters.text: Obtain your API Token from **Settings > Account > User Preferences > API Token** in the BitSight portal.

parameters.content:
## 4. Connection Name

Assign a unique name to identify this connection in the grid and in every ingested log record.

parameters.text: The connection name is stored in the `connectionName` column of every ingested record, enabling you to trace data back to this specific connection.
